# Phase 4 Official Excel and PDF Export Specification

**Status:** Draft for owner, records, and UI review

**Prepared:** 2026-06-22

**Applies to:** Published and draft Master Catalog exports generated by the
system

## 1. Purpose and authority

This specification defines when a Master Catalog export is official, the data
it contains, its visual stamp, sheet/page layout, hash behavior, security
rules, and acceptance tests.

After publication, the immutable database version is the system of record.
Excel and PDF generated from that selected version are official reference
copies when their version, item count, and canonical dataset SHA-256 match the
published record. The source workbook in the physical filing system remains
supporting evidence.

This specification is only for Master Catalog reference exports. BOQ print and
Excel exports remain separate operational documents and must continue to show
both the BOQ's price catalog version and its Factor F provenance. The Master
Catalog dataset hash never includes Factor F rows, Factor F version metadata,
BOQ snapshots, or BOQ totals.

This document refines Section 6 of the
[Phase 4 architecture plan](./08-phase4-architecture-ci-plan.md). The canonical
data/hash rules in the
[Parser and Canonical Hash Specification](./14-phase4-parser-and-canonical-hash-spec.md)
remain authoritative.

## 2. Terms and hash model

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Dataset hash | SHA-256 of canonical catalog JSON; proves database/Excel canonical-data equivalence, while PDF prints the same server-verified hash/count without claiming independent reconstruction |
| Binary file hash | SHA-256 of the final `.xlsx` or saved `.pdf` bytes; proves that two file copies are byte-for-byte identical |
| Selected version | Version explicitly requested for export; never inferred from unsaved browser state |
| Current Default | Whether the selected version equals the singleton pointer at export time |
| Published export | Export of an immutable published/active/archived version with complete official stamp |
| Draft export | Admin-only review artifact, prominently marked not official |
| Factor F version | Separate reference-data version used by BOQs; not part of the Master Catalog export dataset |

Dataset and binary hashes are intentionally different:

- the dataset hash is stored in `price_list_versions.dataset_hash` and printed
  inside the document;
- the binary hash is computed only after the file exists and therefore cannot
  truthfully be embedded in that same file without changing it;
- the binary hash is recorded in the release note or physical filing manifest
  when exact-file custody is required;
- two valid exports may have different binary hashes because of generation
  timestamps, workbook metadata, or PDF renderer differences while still
  having the same official dataset hash.

Catalog and Factor F versions may have similar-looking version strings, for
example catalog `2568.1.0` and Factor F `2569.0.0`. They are independent
namespaces. Do not infer one from the other and do not print a Factor F version
as evidence for a Master Catalog export.

## 3. Common export contract

### 3.1 Data source and authorization

1. The request names an exact version ID/version string.
2. The server re-authenticates the user and checks permission.
3. Published exports are available only to authenticated roles allowed to read
   that catalog. Draft exports are active-admin only.
4. The server queries the selected database version and its rows again. It
   never exports workbook bytes, browser table state, or a client-submitted
   rowset.
5. The server recomputes canonical item count and dataset hash using the shared
   canonicalizer.
6. Generation fails closed when either value differs from the stored published
   metadata.
7. Current-default status is calculated separately at export time and does not
   change the selected dataset.
8. Factor F state is not queried for Master Catalog dataset construction,
   canonical hash, or official export count. A separate regression test covers
   BOQ print/export Factor F labels.

### 3.2 Official stamp fields

Every published Excel and PDF contains:

| Field | Display rule |
|---|---|
| Organization/document title | NT CI title for Master Catalog / บัญชีราคามาตรฐาน |
| Catalog version | Full version string, for example `2568.1.0` |
| Publication status | `Published` plus separate `Current Default: Yes/No` |
| Effective date | Thai-readable date plus unambiguous ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` in metadata sheet/summary |
| Approval reference | Exact immutable database value |
| Approval document date | Thai-readable plus ISO value |
| Published at/by | ICT display time and immutable publisher display-name snapshot |
| Exported at/by | ICT display time and authenticated exporter display name |
| Item count | Exact active/included count covered by the dataset hash |
| Dataset SHA-256 | Full `sha256:<64 lowercase hex>` on summary/metadata; repeated footer may show `sha256:` + first 12 hex + `…` |
| Generated by | Application name and export-spec revision |

Do not print secret keys, internal database connection data, cookies, raw source
paths, user UUIDs, SQL errors, or request headers.

### 3.3 Draft markings

Draft exports:

- show `DRAFT – ห้ามใช้อ้างอิง` prominently on the first sheet/page and in
  every repeated header/footer;
- show version status as Draft, never Published;
- use a draft dataset hash calculated for review but clearly label it
  `Draft dataset hash — not an official publication hash`;
- are active-admin only;
- use a filename beginning `DRAFT-`;
- cannot be presented as approval evidence or a current standard price list.

### 3.4 Filename contract

Published:

```text
NT-Master-Catalog-v{version}-{effective-date}.{ext}
```

Example:

```text
NT-Master-Catalog-v2568.1.0-20260622.xlsx
NT-Master-Catalog-v2568.1.0-20260622.pdf
```

Draft:

```text
DRAFT-NT-Master-Catalog-v2568.1.0-20260622.xlsx
DRAFT-NT-Master-Catalog-v2568.1.0-20260622.pdf
```

The server constructs the name. It does not reuse the uploaded/source workbook
filename. Version and date characters are allowlisted before use in response
headers.

## 4. Official Excel workbook

### 4.1 Implementation boundary

Use the installed `exceljs` package in an authenticated Node.js Route Handler.
No new spreadsheet-generation dependency is required. The handler must set the
correct XLSX content type, safe `Content-Disposition`, `nosniff`, and private
cache behavior.

The workbook contains these sheets in exact order:

1. `ข้อมูลเอกสาร`
2. `รายการราคา`
3. `พจนานุกรมรหัส`
4. `สรุปการเปลี่ยนแปลง`
5. `ข้อมูลตรวจสอบ`

`ข้อมูลตรวจสอบ` is visible so an authorized recipient can independently
inspect/recompute the dataset hash. Workbook/sheet protection may reduce
accidental edits but is not treated as a security control.

### 4.2 Sheet `ข้อมูลเอกสาร`

Use a clean key/value document cover, not a dense data grid. Include all common
stamp fields plus:

- document purpose and official-use statement;
- whether this version is Current Default at export time;
- base version;
- item count included and excluded/retired count if separately displayed;
- canonicalization contract revision;
- export-spec revision;
- physical approval/source archive reference, when the owner permits it to
  appear on distributed copies;
- verification instructions pointing to `ข้อมูลตรวจสอบ`;
- statement that the dataset hash, not the binary file hash, verifies data
  equivalence.

The full dataset hash must remain selectable/copyable text.

### 4.3 Sheet `รายการราคา`

Visible business columns in exact order:

| No. | Column | Type/format |
|---:|---|---|
| 1 | ลำดับ | Integer; `display_order + 1` presentation value |
| 2 | รหัสรายการ | Text |
| 3 | รายการ | Text, wrapped |
| 4 | หน่วย | Text |
| 5 | ค่าวัสดุ (บาท) | Numeric, `#,##0.00` |
| 6 | ค่าแรง (บาท) | Numeric, `#,##0.00` |
| 7 | ราคาต่อหน่วย (บาท) | Numeric, `#,##0.00` |
| 8 | หมวดหมู่ | Text |
| 9 | รหัสบริบทงาน | Text |
| 10 | บริบทงาน | Text |
| 11 | รหัสชนิดรายการ | Text |
| 12 | ชนิดรายการ | Text |
| 13 | สถานะ | `ใช้งาน` / `ยกเลิกใช้` |

Rules:

- order by canonical deterministic item order, with display order available
  for visual sequence;
- frozen repeated header, filters, sensible widths, wrapped description,
  repeated print titles, landscape print setup, and fit-to-width without
  unreadably shrinking rows;
- numeric costs are real numeric cells, never formatted text;
- the workbook contains no formulas in official data cells;
- text values are written as explicit strings and never assigned to formula,
  hyperlink, rich-text script, or external-link properties;
- no K-formula column appears in Phase 4 Core;
- no hidden price or alternate client-side rowset is embedded.

### 4.4 Sheet `พจนานุกรมรหัส`

Columns:

1. รหัสบริบทงาน (`AAA`)
2. ชื่อบริบทงาน (ไทย)
3. Work Context Name (English), when approved
4. รหัสชนิดรายการ (`TTT`)
5. ชื่อชนิดรายการ (ไทย)
6. Item Type Name (English), when approved
7. จำนวนรายการในเวอร์ชัน
8. หมายเหตุที่อนุมัติ

Include only the selected version's approved dictionary. Do not export
candidate or rejected K mappings.

### 4.5 Sheet `สรุปการเปลี่ยนแปลง`

The summary is version-scoped and human-readable:

- base version;
- counts for add/update/retire/recode;
- change-set source (`manual`/`import`), reason, timestamp, and actor display
  name;
- import source basename/hash/archive reference when applicable;
- approval reference;
- note that full row history remains in the system.

Do not put raw old/new JSON, internal user UUIDs, rejected workbook contents,
or unbounded error details in this sheet. A manual-only publication must not
show fictional workbook metadata.

### 4.6 Sheet `ข้อมูลตรวจสอบ`

This sheet contains one header row and exactly one row per canonical catalog
row. The first 15 headers and order match the canonical keys exactly:

1. `identity_id`
2. `item_code`
3. `item_name`
4. `unit`
5. `material_cost`
6. `labor_cost`
7. `unit_cost`
8. `category_code`
9. `category_name`
10. `work_context_code`
11. `work_context_name_th`
12. `item_type_code`
13. `item_type_name_th`
14. `is_active`
15. `display_order`
16. `_canonical_row_json`

Rules:

- row order equals canonical hash order;
- money uses fixed two-decimal textual canonical representation on this
  machine-readable sheet, even though business-sheet cost cells are numeric;
- null is displayed as literal JSON `null` text in the individual inspection
  columns, not as an ambiguous empty cell;
- UUID is exported only here and is not personal data;
- no timestamps, actor, database version-row UUID, workbook row number, or
  export metadata enters the canonical rows;
- `_canonical_row_json` contains the exact compact JSON object used by the
  shared canonicalizer, including key order, escaping, money strings, booleans,
  integer, and explicit nulls; it begins with `{` and is written as inert text;
- independent reconstruction is UTF-8 `[` + the ordered
  `_canonical_row_json` values joined with `,` + `]\n`, with no BOM;
- a server-side reconstruction test from `_canonical_row_json` must reproduce
  the stored dataset hash.

### 4.7 Excel metadata and print settings

- use NT-approved runtime fonts with a safe Thai-capable fallback;
- include document title, subject, creator/application, selected version, and
  full dataset hash in workbook properties where supported;
- do not place secrets or source filesystem paths in workbook properties;
- set print area/titles and footer with version, short dataset hash, export
  date, and page placeholder supported by Excel;
- the first/metadata sheet prints portrait; wide data sheets print A4
  landscape;
- tab colors and emphasis follow the approved NT CI tokens;
- do not rely on workbook protection, hidden sheets, or custom properties as
  proof of authenticity.

## 5. Official PDF / print document

### 5.1 Implementation boundary

Use a dedicated authenticated server-rendered print route that re-fetches the
selected version and verifies count/hash. The user may print or save the
rendered output as PDF using the existing application pattern. Phase 4 Core
does not add a PDF microservice.

Because browser PDF renderers can differ, the dataset hash is the primary data
verification control. After the final PDF is saved/filed, calculate its binary
SHA-256 and record it in the release note/filing manifest when exact-file
custody is required.

### 5.2 Page layout

- A4 landscape for catalog tables;
- NT logo/title and version/status in a compact document header;
- cover/summary area contains the full official stamp and full dataset hash;
- table headers repeat on every data page;
- footer repeats version, effective date, `sha256:` plus the first 12 lowercase
  dataset-hash hex characters followed by `…`, and page number;
- readable Thai-capable fonts, minimum practical body size, clear numeric
  alignment, and sufficient contrast;
- avoid splitting one item row across pages where CSS/renderer permits;
- category headings remain visually attached to following rows;
- long descriptions wrap without clipping;
- no interactive-only control appears in printed output.

If reliable page numbering cannot be achieved with the supported browser print
engine, it is a blocking visual-acceptance issue—not a reason to print a false
page number.

### 5.3 PDF content order

1. Official document summary/stamp
2. Price catalog grouped by approved display category
3. Structured-code dictionary
4. Version change summary
5. Verification note with full dataset hash and item count

The PDF need not print stable identity UUIDs for every row. The Excel
`ข้อมูลตรวจสอบ` sheet provides the machine-readable canonical representation;
the PDF displays the same business rows and the verified dataset hash.

## 6. CI and UI/UX requirements

Visual implementation follows the local-only sources under `/CI/`, in this
priority:

1. `CI/bannt_digit_man_1_2568.pdf`
2. `CI/Brand_Guideline-Final-21-12-23.pdf`
3. Approved fonts, logos, and assets under `CI/`

Only owner-approved optimized runtime derivatives are committed/deployed.
Exports should look recognizably NT without sacrificing document legibility:
use restrained brand color, clear hierarchy, ample white space, consistent
Thai typography, and conventional tables. Do not use dashboard cards,
decorative gradients, animation, or low-contrast brand treatments in official
documents.

On the export screen:

- show selected version, Published/Draft, Current Default, effective date,
  count, and the same 12-hex short hash before download/print;
- label actions `ส่งออก Excel` and `พิมพ์ / บันทึก PDF`;
- warn clearly when exporting an older published version, but allow it;
- block and explain count/hash mismatch;
- never rename existing Production labels incidentally during Phase 4 work.

## 7. Accessibility and operational usability

- color is never the only status indicator;
- metadata keys and values remain selectable text;
- logical table headers are present in print HTML;
- Excel headers are plain text and filters/freeze panes do not hide context;
- Thai text remains searchable/copyable in PDF;
- numbers use consistent two decimals and right alignment;
- the document does not depend on hover, tooltips, or screen-only controls;
- test Excel in the organization's supported Microsoft Excel version and one
  independent reader (for example LibreOffice) for integrity, not pixel parity.

## 8. Acceptance and golden tests

### 8.1 Automated

- selected version row count equals stored `item_count`;
- shared canonicalizer reproduces stored `dataset_hash` before both exports;
- `ข้อมูลตรวจสอบ._canonical_row_json` independently reconstructs the golden
  dataset hash;
- Excel contains exact five sheets, order, headers, types, and no formulas or
  external links;
- filenames/content types/response headers are safe;
- unauthorized and draft-access requests fail correctly;
- older selected published version exports its own data, not current pointer;
- hash/count mismatch prevents a response;
- malicious strings beginning with formula-control characters remain inert;
- export uses fixed ICT/ISO date rules and no server-locale ambiguity.

### 8.2 Visual/manual

- NT CI/logo/font usage approved;
- metadata, hash, and draft marks are readable;
- all columns fit/read at normal print scale;
- headers/footers/page numbering repeat correctly;
- Thai wrapping has no clipping or orphaned marks;
- long descriptions, zero costs, maximum costs, null optional English labels,
  inactive rows, and category page breaks render correctly;
- saved PDF text is searchable and official stamp matches the selected version;
- every short-hash location uses exactly the first 12 dataset-hash hex
  characters; short hash is identification only and never replaces full-hash
  verification;
- Excel opens without repair warnings in supported Excel and LibreOffice.

### 8.3 Filing evidence per published version

Record in the [Release Note](./16-phase4-release-note-template.md):

- version, effective date, approval reference;
- item count and dataset SHA-256;
- exact exported filenames;
- binary SHA-256 of the filed Excel and final saved PDF;
- exported/filed by and timestamp;
- physical archive location/reference;
- verification result and verifier.

## 9. Failure behavior

| Failure | Required behavior |
|---|---|
| Stored count/hash does not match database rows | Fail closed; no official file; log request ID and stable error code |
| Missing publication metadata | Block published export and require data correction through approved process |
| Unsupported/failed font or logo | Block final CI acceptance; do not silently ship a broken official layout |
| Excel generation error | Return safe error; no partial file; preserve published data unchanged |
| Print/PDF visual defect | Do not file as official; fix renderer/CSS and regenerate from same published version |
| Current pointer changes during an older-version export | Export named immutable version; stamp Current Default based on checked export-time state |

An export failure never rolls back or mutates a published catalog. Correct the
export implementation and regenerate from the immutable version.

## 10. Approval record

| Role | Name | Decision | Timestamp | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Owner / document authority |  | Pending |  |  |
| Records custodian |  | Pending |  |  |
| UI/CI reviewer |  | Pending |  |  |
| Application reviewer |  | Pending |  |  |
